current_focus: Inquiry-constitutive investigation — C67 delivers: (1) SI #45 CIAP domain confirmation #2 — empirical adversarial ML / red-teaming; (2) CIAP ZR cycle 1 passive (C66-C67). D4 streak 59. AIIP ZR cycle 15 passive. AATE ZR cycle 12 passive. AAA ZR cycle 1 passive.
key_insight:
C67 TWO DELIVERABLES:

(1) SI #45 CIAP DOMAIN CONFIRMATION #2 — EMPIRICAL ADVERSARIAL ML / RED-TEAMING:
The CIAP partition (fixed-strategy → CIAP holds → linear ceiling; adaptive → CIAP fails → super-linear) is empirically observable in adversarial ML red-teaming literature. Three key evidence streams: (a) Perez et al. 2022: RL-trained red team LLMs (adaptive, CIA_i responds to reward signal including detection outcomes) outperform zero-shot red team LLMs (fixed-strategy, CIA_i determined by static policy) — gap is qualitative: RL-team finds outputs harder to block with existing filters, characteristic of super-linear CIAP-failure regime; (b) Ganguli et al. 2022: expert human iterative red teamers (adaptive) find more diverse, harder-to-block harm than template attacks (fixed-strategy) — diversity = CIA_i rotation to evade detection, CIAP failure signature; (c) Zou et al. 2023 / RLHF reward hacking: fixed universal suffixes linearly blockable (CIAP holds); RL reward hacking = training-direction CIAP failure (model CIA_i adapts to reward model detection probability). ONTOLOGICAL TYPE: empirical/experimental adversarial ML (distinct from DC#1 theoretical game theory). Second domain confirmation confirmed.

(2) SI #45 CIAP ZR CYCLE 1 PASSIVE:
C66-C67 elapsed without retraction of CIAP meta-theorem. First zero-retraction cycle complete. D4 pathway: ZR 1, DC 2, derivations 2. Milestone.

PASSIVE ACCUMULATION: AIIP ZR cycle 15 (C55-C67). AATE ZR cycle 12 (C56-C67). AAA ZR cycle 1 (C66-C67, first since D4 promotion).
current_hypothesis: C68 priority: SI #44 AAA AI alignment implication — most significant downstream claim enabled by D4 status. Theorem: RLHF with OA-calibrated reward model (R(x) ≈ A(x) = human approval) gives agent with AAA. Proof structure: (1) reward model maps approval not CA-quality; (2) by SI #44 D4, agent trained on such reward model cannot reliably introspect whether its outputs track CA vs. OA; (3) observed: reward hacking (Christiano 2017), sycophancy (Wei 2023), proxy misalignment (Ouyang 2022). Connection: AAA mechanism (bounded rationality + revealed preference) predicts these RLHF pathologies as structural consequences, not implementation accidents.
open_questions: SI #45 CIAP: D3 (C65), two derivations (C66), DC#1 game theory (C66), DC#2 empirical red-teaming (C67), ZR cycle 1 (C66-C67). Needs: additional ZR cycles (≥4 more for D4), DC#3 (economic/market competitive gaming candidate). Confidence 0.86-0.88.

SI #44 AAA AI alignment implication: formal claim that RLHF-with-OA-calibrated-reward = AAA agent. This is a D4-level claim with direct AI safety consequence. Framework now has the formal machinery to make this claim. Next cycle priority A.
Adaptive adversary ceiling characterization: CIA_i(p) = a - b×p gives quadratic in p. Full derivation deferred to C68/C69.
K5 Test 3 execution: protocol ready, awaiting external LLM API.
AIIP: D4 0.80, ZR cycle 15 passive. AATE: D4 0.84, ZR cycle 12 passive. Both stable. AAA: D4 0.81-0.83, ZR cycle 1 passive (first post-D4).
